items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin trades above $65,000 as Alphabet's bigger AI bill props up the chip trade
items.description: Alphabet beat on revenue but raised its AI spending forecast again, sending its stock lower after hours. For the chip complex bitcoin tracks, more spending is the bullish read.

items.title: BitMEX, the exchange that invented perps, is shutting down
items.description: BitMEX, co-founded by Arthur Hayes in 2014, redefined the global crypto market by introducing the 100x leverage perpetual swap.

items.title: 24/7 financial rails: How BNY plans to eliminate the weekend lag in U.S. Treasuries
items.description: The bank will begin testing tokenized Treasuries on a private blockchain by year-end and expand its settlement network to cover more international trading hours.

items.title: South Korea's oldest crypto exchange is now part of the $1 trillion Mirae Group family
items.description: Korbit, South Korea's first cryptocurrency exchange, has joined the Mirae Asset Group family, marking the first time a traditional Korean financial group's affiliate has taken a controlling stake in a domestic crypto exchange.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum-linked protocols lose $35 million in multiple attacks hours apart
items.description: The attacks hit Verus, B² Network and other cross-chain systems, showing how compromised keys, upgrade powers and validation checks can empty protocols without breaking the underlying cryptography.

items.title: Arbitrum-based AFX Trade drained of $24 million after bridge keys compromised
items.description: Security firms said the attacker used enough hot-validator signatures to approve a 24.15 million USDC withdrawal, while Arbitrum said its native bridge was not affected.

items.title: XRP whales accumulate as small holders capitulate
items.description: XRP whales accumulate 2.8% more tokens over five weeks as small holders capitulate, sending the price back above $1.16.

items.title: Bitcoin wilts as oil and rates rise. Clarity Act odds tumble to 38%
items.description: Geopolitical risks, rising rates, and fresh regulatory setbacks send crypto lower as key Democrats demand stronger safeguards in the market structure bill.

items.title: Bitcoin may have bottomed before its traditional cycle low: Grayscale’s Pandl
items.description: Grayscale’s head of research said that Bitcoin may have bottomed, arguing that macro factors, such as interest rate decisions as increasingly driving the price action of this maturing asset class.

items.title: Home invasions became most common crypto wrench attack in H1 2026: CertiK
items.description: Crypto home invasions rose to 20 in H1 2026 from one a year earlier, while France accounted for 33 of 52 verified wrench attacks, according to CertiK.

items.title: BitMEX to shut down after 11 years in crypto derivatives
items.description: BitMEX announced it will shut down operations on Sept. 23, 2026, after owner HDR Global Trading Limited decided to close the crypto exchange.

items.title: Mirae Asset completes Korbit acquisition, becomes largest shareholder
items.description: Mirae Asset Consulting has become Korbit’s largest shareholder after completing its acquisition, with the exchange saying trading and customer asset protections are unchanged.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs approach $1B in 7-session inflow run
items.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $69 million in inflows on Wednesday, extending their inflow streak to seven sessions and bringing total inflows during the period to nearly $1 billion

items.title: Kakao taps Circle to explore won stablecoin payment infrastructure
items.description: Kakao Group and Circle signed an MOU to explore won stablecoin payments, remittances, merchant settlement and tokenized financial services.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Swiss bank BancaStato launches regulated crypto trading with Sygnum
items.description: Swiss cantonal bank BancaStato integrated Sygnum’s crypto trading and custody services into its Avaloq-powered digital banking apps for Bitcoin and other assets.

items.title: Houthis claim strikes on Saudi Arabian oil tankers as Rubio says Iran not ready to make a deal
items.description: Yemen's Houthis said they had targeted two Saudi Arabian oil tankers with drones and missiles for allegedly violating its maritime blockade.

items.title: Tesla and Alphabet shares slump in premarket trading as AI spending concerns spook investors
items.description: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Oil tops $98 after tankers struck off Saudi Arabia, Trump escalates Iran threats
items.description: Oil prices rose after U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure in response to any attacks on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: UniCredit CEO tells CNBC acquisition of Commerzbank could happen in fourth quarter
items.description: UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel told CNBC Thursday that the Italian bank could "potentially" acquire Commerzbank in the fourth quarter.

items.title: Silver and gold prices stage a cautious rebound — but analysts see slim hopes for a sustained rally
items.description: Precious metals have rallied in recent days, but analysts say prices could face a difficult path back to all-time highs achieved earlier this year.

items.title: Hedge funds circle UK stocks as new PM Andy Burnham pledges 'new economic model'
items.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham's overhaul of Britain's domestic policy could fuel a range of trading opportunities in U.K. stocks.

items.title: Why we're sticking with Alphabet despite an imperfect quarter and more AI spending
items.description: The market isn't in a forgiving mood, but we're not in a hurry to leave this stock behind.

items.title: Tesla misses on earnings, as free cash flow turns negative and margins slide
items.description: Tesla's earnings report lands in the midst of a slide in the company's stock price even as its core auto business is rebounding.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VlfsV2wvdhk! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: The late James Van Der Beek’s former Beverly Hills home up for rent for $13,950 a month
items.description: Late “Dawson’s Creek” star James Van Der Beek’s former Beverly Hills home has been offered up for rent—five months after the actor died following a battle with cancer.

items.title: I will definitely claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
items.description: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

items.title: Texas Instruments shares are sliding, and its rival is doing even worse. What’s going on in the world of analog semiconductors.
items.description: Texas Instruments shares were falling while STMicroelectronics stock was diving on Thursday, after both makers of analog semiconductors struggled to meet heightened expectations after reporting strong demand in the second quarter.

items.title: The bull market faces higher likelihood of a Fed rate hike as Iran crisis intensifies
items.description: Treasury yields are approaching the highest levels seen since the Iran war started in February as oil prices rise

items.title: ServiceNow’s stock rises as earnings show momentum in cybersecurity
items.description: Against a gloomy backdrop for software sentiment, ServiceNow just topped revenue expectations.

items.title: IBM just cut its outlook, but not by as much as investors feared
items.description: IBM’s official earnings report comes after a bruising profit warning last week.

items.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?
items.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

items.title: I want to transfer $17,000 in credit-card debt. Why did Wells Fargo offer me only a $4,000 credit limit?
items.description: “I asked why the limit was so low, but they couldn’t give me an explanation.”

items.title: Oil jumps to $98 after Houthis attack two Saudi Arabian tankers
items.description: Yemeni rebels say they used missiles and drones to target the vessels Encelia and Layla

items.title: Burnham announces cut to business rates for pubs and clubs
items.description: Prime minister says move will help boost high streets across UK

items.title: Modi vows ‘swift’ justice for India exam leaks
items.description: Prime minister makes first comments on scandal that triggered protests in Delhi this week

items.title: BASF warns EU carbon plans risk accelerating industrial decline
items.description: Also in this newsletter: Death during police arrest fuels security debate in Italy

items.title: A practical problem with Andy Burnham’s postcode power-up plan
items.description: We need stats, stat

items.title: FirstFT: Google burns through $6bn in cash amid AI infrastructure splurge
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Houthis attack two Saudi tankers and Jes Staley on Capitol Hill

items.title: Household inflation expectations in the US are worryingly high
items.description: Much higher inflation expectations may well require action by the Fed, even as the market remains sanguine

items.title: English football is for sale
items.description: In the afterglow of the World Cup, some club owners are looking to do deals

items.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
items.description: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

items.title: As Cyclosporiasis Spreads, America Says No Thanks to Salads
items.description: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

items.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
items.description: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

items.title: Global Oil Price Nears $100 a Barrel as Conflict With Iran Deepens
items.description: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
items.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.

items.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
items.description: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

items.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
items.description: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

items.title: Tesla Profit Falls Even as Car Sales Rebound
items.description: The company is selling more cars, but the company’s profit was down because of price cuts and higher expenses.
